zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Isabel Elizabeth Barnby於1375年5月1日出生在英國越可爾卡素 霍華德時，她的父親Edmund Widdrington51歲，母親Lady Widdrington50歲。她於大約1391年與William de Lascelles在英國結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1460年在她的家鄉享年85歲，埋葬在英國越可爾卡素 霍華德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Isabel Elizabeth Barnby于1375年5月1日出生在英国越可爾卡素 霍華德时，她的父亲Edmund Widdrington51岁，母亲Lady Widdrington50岁。她于大约1391年与William de Lascelles在英国结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1460年在她的家乡享年85岁，埋葬在英国越可爾卡素 霍華德。

nameMeanings.name: Barnby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: variant of   Barmby   or   Barnaby  .

variants.name: Barnaby
meanings: from the Middle English vernacular form of the Greek and Latin personal name Barnabas, which was borne by the companion of St. Paul (Acts 4:36). This is of Aramaic origin, from Aramaic bar naḅyā ‘son of the prophet’, although the Greek text of Acts 4:36 explains the name as hyios paraklēseōs ‘son of consolation’ or ‘encouragement’.

habitational name from any of the four places: Barnaby in North Yorkshire, Barnetby le Wold or Barnoldby le Beck (both in Lincolnshire), or Barnby in Suffolk. Barnaby in North Yorkshire is named with the Old English personal name Beornwald (composed of the elements beorn ‘young warrior’ + wald ‘rule’) + Old Norse bȳ ‘settlement, farmstead’. Barnby in Suffolk is named with Old Norse barn ‘child, offspring’ + bȳ meaning ‘children's farm’, perhaps for an estate divided by heirs.

variants.name: Barmby
meanings: English: locative name from Barmby Moor or Barmby on the Marsh (both ER Yorks), or Barnby in Lythe (NR Yorks), which may also have contributed to   Barnaby  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Isabel Elizabeth Barnby was born on 1 May 1375, in Castle Howard, Yorkshire, England, her father, Edmund Widdrington, was 51 and her mother, Lady Widdrington, was 50. She married William de Lascelles about 1391, in England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1460, in her hometown, at the age of 85, and was buried in Castle Howard, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Isabel Elizabeth Barnby est née le 1 mai 1375, à Castle Howard, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Widdrington, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Lady Widdrington, avait 50 ans. Elle a épousé William de Lascelles environ 1391, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1460, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterrée à Castle Howard, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Isabel Elizabeth Barnby am 1. Mai 1375 in Castle Howard, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edmund Widdrington 51 und ihre Mutter Lady Widdrington 50. Sie hat um 1391 in England William de Lascelles geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1460 im Alter von 85 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Castle Howard, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabel Elizabeth Barnby è nata il il 1 maggio 1375 nel Castle Howard, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Edmund Widdrington, aveva 51 anni e sua madre, Lady Widdrington, aveva 50 anni. Lei sposato William de Lascelles circa 1391, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1460, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 85 anni, e fu sepolta a Castle Howard, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel Elizabeth Barnbyが1375年5月1日にイギリスヨークシュアイアカストル ホーワードで生まれた時，父Edmund Widdringtonは51歳，母Lady Widdringtonは50歳であった。約1391年にイギリスでWilliam de Lascellesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1460年に故郷で85歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアカストル ホーワードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel Elizabeth Barnby(이)가 1375년 5월 1일 영국 요르크실르 카스틀 하워드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Widdrington(은)는 51세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Widdrington(은)는 50세였다. 그녀는 대략 1391년 영국에서 William de Lascelles(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1460년 그녀의 고향에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 카스틀 하워드에 묻혔다.

mn.Mickleton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino: Mickleton, Eoferwic, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabel Elizabeth Barnby nasceu em 1 de maio de 1375, em Castle Howard, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund Widdrington, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Lady Widdrington, tinha 50 anos. Ela casou-se com William de Lascelles aproximadamente 1391, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1460, em sua cidade natal, com 85 anos, e foi sepultada em Castle Howard, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Isabel Elizabeth Barnby родилась 1 мая 1375 г. в местности кастл хоуард, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund Widdrington было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Widdrington, 50. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Lascelles около 1391 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1460 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 85 лет и была похоронена в местности кастл хоуард, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Isabel Elizabeth Barnby nació el 1 de mayo de 1375, en Castle Howard, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Edmund Widdrington, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Lady Widdrington, tenía 50 años. Se casó con William de Lascelles aproximadamente 1391, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1460, en su pueblo, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultada en Castle Howard, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
